title: JavaScript Cookies Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with cookies and storage.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript for storing small text files in a browser?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using HTTP Cookies (like document.cookie) to store session information.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using HttpOnly cookies compared to regular ones?
options: It makes your HTML files smaller and more readable.
It prevents JavaScript from reading the cookie, making it much harder for malicious scripts to steal it.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'document.cookie' inside a professional web application?
options: To make the site load much faster.
A long string containing all the cookies for a specific website, which you can parse and use in your JS.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS use 'Secure' cookies in modern production environments?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They ensure that the cookie is only sent over an encrypted HTTPS connection to prevent hackers from seeing it.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for JavaScript cookies?
options: Commenting every single line of code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Using 'HttpOnly' and 'Secure' cookies for any sensitive user information (like session IDs).
Using variables for colors and spacing.
